ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Mar-25-2008 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Lawrence Siracusa for Bertha Joung (Special Administrator).

Ms. Siracusa stated that this was a difficult case, that the Public Administrator had turned it down, that his client was nuetral and in the middle of both sides, and that the matter involved dealing with litigation counsel for the real property. In addition, Mr. Siracusa reported that the request for fees had been presented to Counsel for the administrator and that they had entered into a private settlement agreement with the Beneficiary to pay reduced fees. The Court took the matter under submission.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Clerk: Rosie Noguera.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jan-09-2007 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Mr. Fox (Counsel for Archie Kwong); Lawrence Siracusa (Counsel fo Bertha Joung, Special Administrator); Charles Triay (Counsel for Hon Wah Kwong, Objector).

Counsel reported on the status of the Hong Kong probate proceedings, noting that the decedent's Will had been admitted into probate there and that an ancillary probate proceeding would be initiated here. No further hearing date was set as the filing of the ancillary proceeding would give rise to the next hearing date.

Judicial Officer: Judge John Dearman; Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR #11722; Court Examiner: Tim Kneis; Bailiff: Deputy Mizota; Clerk: R. Michael Diles.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION, APPOINTMENT UNDER IAEA
Off Calendar Jul-12-2006 Continued To Jan-09-07 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Jul-10-2006 Written Order Received
MINI MINUTES: Doug Penson appearing for and with special administrator Bertha Joung. Michael Fox appearing for Archie Kwong. Charles Triay appearing for and with Hon Wah Kwong, aka Eddy Kwong. His Hong Kong counsel T.C. Liu is also in court. Parties are having settlement discussion in Hong Kong. Trial date is set on 1/15/07 in Hong Kong. Eddy Kwong is willing to lend $10,000.00 to the estate to repair the property. He will be reimbursed when the case is resolved. Mr. Penson will prepare an order and send it to other counsel for approval as to form. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-04-2006 Continued To Jun-13-06 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Michael Fox appearing for Archie Wong. Randy Schmidt appearing for Eddy Kwong. Douglas Penson appearing for and with Special Administrator. Status on Hong Kong reported. Case is on appeal of a Discovery order. The special administrator was not able to obtain a permit to repair the lower unit. It is not a legal unit. She wants to get landlord tenant counsel for advise. Mr. Fox wants to sell the property. Mr. Schmidt does not want to sell. The Court wants parties to work out their concerns and to remedy the problem. Matter is continued to 6/13. Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin, CSR 11722. Clerk: Ella Yip.

Petition For General Powers To Special Administrator
Off Calendar Aug-22-2005 Denied
PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order To Compel Deponent To Attend And Estify At Deposition And Produce For Inspection Documents And/Or Tangible Things Described In The Deposition
Off Calendar Jul-11-2005 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearance:
Paul Young Choi, for Petitioner/moving party
No appearance for Respondent

Ruling:
Tentative Ruling adopted: Petitioner's Motion To Compel Deponent To Attend and Testify At Deposition and To Produce For Inspection Documents and/or Tangible Things Described In The Deposition Noticed is granted. There is no merit to the argument that this court has no jurisdiction and that the appointment of an administrator made the noticed deposition beyond the scope of the proceedings. Petitioner's request for sanctions is denied as there is no declaration supporting the amount of any monetary sanction sought. [C.C.P. Section 2023.040]
The objections to the purported Verified Statement of Facts are sustained in part and overruled in part as follows: the objections to Paragraphs 2, 4, 11, 16, 17, 19, 22, and 23 are overruled; the objections to Paragraph 3 is sustained; the hearsay objection is sustained as to Paragraphs 7, 8, 9, and 10 are sustained; the hearsay objection is sustained as to the last sentence of Paragraphs 5 and 12. COMM: EVERETT A. HEWLETT
